Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse 531 upvotes, 74 comments
  2. Optimizing Top K in Postgres 106 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. Show HN: Remotely use my guitar tuner 223 upvotes, 49 comments
  4. FreeBSD 14.4-Release Announcement 53 upvotes, 9 comments
  5. Graphing how the 10k* most common English words define each other 92 upvotes, 21 comments
  6. JSLinux Now Supports x86_64 345 upvotes, 109 comments
  7. Caxlsx: Ruby gem for xlsx generation with charts, images, schema validation 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Two Years of Emacs Solo 293 upvotes, 95 comments
  9. LoGeR – 3D reconstruction from extremely long videos (DeepMind, UC Berkeley) 85 upvotes, 22 comments
  10. Darkrealms BBS 116 upvotes, 33 comments
  11. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" (2009) 118 upvotes, 35 comments
  12. I put my whole life into a single database 173 upvotes, 58 comments
  13. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 19 upvotes, 3 comments
  14. Lotus 1-2-3 on the PC with DOS 131 upvotes, 48 comments
  15. TCXO Failure Analysis 50 upvotes, 15 comments
  16. Practical Guide to Bare Metal C++ 36 upvotes, 12 comments
  17. The hidden compile-time cost of C++26 reflection 46 upvotes, 18 comments
  18. The “JVG algorithm” only wins on tiny numbers 76 upvotes, 41 comments
  19. Yann LeCun's AI startup raises $1B in Europe's largest ever seed round 150 upvotes, 97 comments
  20. No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user 307 upvotes, 226 comments
  21. macOS Tahoe windows have different corner radiuses 232 upvotes, 169 comments
  22. Launch HN: Terminal Use (YC W26) – Vercel for filesystem-based agents 110 upvotes, 76 comments
  23. Ask HN: Remember Fidonet? 53 upvotes, 32 comments
  24. Learnings from paying artists royalties for AI-generated art 155 upvotes, 125 comments
  25. Show HN: DenchClaw – Local CRM on Top of OpenClaw 128 upvotes, 108 comments
  26. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 235 upvotes, 219 comments
  27. Show HN: I Was Here – Draw on street view, others can find your drawings 47 upvotes, 34 comments
  28. Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft 512 upvotes, 518 comments
  29. DARPA’s new X-76 209 upvotes, 198 comments
  30. EVi, a Hard-Fork of Vim 19 upvotes, 13 comments

Last generated 9:30pm, 10 March 2026. Source here.
